fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of the service document on the line.

fields.name: Customer No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the customer who owns the items in the service document.

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Bill-to Customer No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the customer that you send or sent the invoice or credit memo to.

fields.name: Bill-to Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the customer that you send or sent the invoice or credit memo to.

fields.name: Bill-to Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the name of the customer that you send or sent the invoice or credit memo to.

fields.name: Bill-to Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address of the customer to whom you will send the credit memo.

fields.name: Bill-to Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional line of the address.

fields.name: Bill-to City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city of the address.

fields.name: Bill-to Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the contact person at the customer's billing address.

fields.name: Your Reference
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's reference. The content will be printed on the related document.

fields.name: Ship-to Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for an alternate shipment address if you want to ship to another address than the one that has been entered automatically. This field is also used in case of drop shipment.

fields.name: Ship-to Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the customer at the address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Ship-to Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of thethe name of the customer at the address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Ship-to Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Ship-to Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the ship-to address, in case it is a long address.

fields.name: Ship-to City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city of the address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Ship-to Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the contact person at the address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Order Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the related order was created.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the service document should be posted.

fields.name: Posting Description
fields.tooltip:
Contains the document type and number of the entry (Order 1001, for example). The program automatically creates the posting description when you fill in the Document Type and No. fields.
The posting description will appear on all customer and G/L entries created from the service document.

fields.name: Payment Terms Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a formula that calculates the payment due date, payment discount date, and payment discount amount.

fields.name: Due Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the related invoice must be paid.

fields.name: Payment Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of payment discount given, if the customer pays by the date entered in the Pmt. Discount Date field.

fields.name: Pmt. Discount Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date on which the amount in the entry must be paid for a payment discount to be granted.

fields.name: Shipment Method Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the delivery conditions of the related shipment, such as free on board (FOB).

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the location (for example, warehouse or distribution center) of the items specified on the service item lines. When you select the customer and the customer has a location assigned, the value is taken from the Customer card. If the customer has no location, but a Responsibility Center is populated, the location code is taken from the Responsibility Center. If neither is specified, the value is taken from Company Information.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Customer Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's market type to link business transactions to.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the currency code for various amounts on the service lines.

fields.name: Currency Factor
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a currency factor if an additional reporting currency was set in the General Ledger Setup window. The currency factor shows the relationship between the additional reporting currency and the local currency. The program will automatically record amounts in both LCY and the additional reporting currency, using the relevant exchange rate and the currency factor.
The program validates the currency factor against the exchange rate for the additional reporting currency as of the posting date. If you have not set up any additional reporting currency, the value in the field will equal 1.

fields.name: Customer Price Group
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code of the price group assigned to the customer. To see the customer price group codes available in the Customer Price Group table, choose the field.
The program uses the code to calculate the unit price for items on the service lines. Unit prices for an item may vary for different price groups. You can enter them in the Sales Prices window. The program will then check the Sales Price table to see whether the customer in the certain price group will be charged a different item price than the item's standard unit price.
The program automatically retrieves the code from the Customer table when you fill in the Bill-to Customer No. field. The field is blank if you have assigned no price group code to the customer.

fields.name: Prices Including VAT
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the Unit Price and Line Amount fields on document lines should be shown with or without VAT.

fields.name: Invoice Disc. Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code used to find the invoice discount you give the customer.
The program retrieves the invoice discount code from the Customer table when you fill in the Bill-to Customer No. field.
The invoice discount will be based on the total invoice amount, but it will be allocated to the lines relative to the amount on each. If there is a check mark in the Calc. Inv. Discount field in the Service Mgt. Setup window, the amount will be calculated automatically. Click here to learn when the automatic calculation will occur.
If the Calc. Inv. Discount field in the Service Mgt. Setup window is not selected, you must activate the calculation manually.
You set up the code in the Invoice Disc. Code field on the customer card.

fields.name: Customer Disc. Group
fields.tooltip:
Contains the discount group assigned to the bill-to customer. The code is used to calculate the service line discount given to the customer.
The program copies the code from the Customer table when you fill in the Bill-to Customer No. field.

fields.name: Language Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a code that determines the language to be used on any printouts for the bill-to customer on the service document.
The program copies the code from the Customer table when you fill in the Bill-to Customer No. field.
The language code allows you to have the items on the service lines written in the customer's language. When you enter an item on the line, the program uses the language code to check whether there is a description of the item in the appropriate language. If there is, the program automatically uses it to replace the usual text on the service item line.

fields.name: Salesperson Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the salesperson assigned to this service document.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip:
Indicates whether there are any comments attached to the current service document.
To view a comment or create a new one, click Related Information, point to Order, and then click Comment. A window then appears which displays existing comments and allows new ones to be created.
You can also enter a comment in the Notes FactBox.

fields.name: No. Printed
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of times that the service order has been printed.
This field is automatically updated every time that the credit memo is printed.

fields.name: Applies-to Doc. Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of the posted document that this document or journal line will be applied to when you post, for example to register payment.

fields.name: Applies-to Doc. No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the posted document that this document or journal line will be applied to when you post, for example to register payment.

fields.name: Bal. Account No.
fields.tooltip:
Contains the number of a general ledger account or the bank account where to place a balancing entry for the invoice. You can select the appropriate type in the Bal. Account Type field.
You can use a search name to enter an account. If you want to enter the cash account, for example, but do not remember the account number, you can enter the word cash and press Enter. The program will enter the correct account number if you have set up CASH as the search name for the account.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of amounts on all the lines in the document.

fields.name: Amount Including VAT
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of amounts, including VAT, on all the lines in the document.

fields.name: Shipping No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number that will be assigned to the shipment when the service header is posted.
When you post the service header, the program will automatically use the next number from the number series in the Shipping No. Series field unless you manually enter a number.
If you manually enter a number, it will not cause a gap in the number series.

fields.name: Posting No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the posted invoice or credit memo that will be created if you post the service header.
When you post the service header, the program will automatically use the next number from the number series in the Posting No. Series field unless you manually enter a number.
If you manually enter a number, it will not cause a gap in the number series.

fields.name: Last Shipping No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the last posted service shipment number used.

fields.name: Last Posting No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the last posted invoice or credit memo number used.

fields.name: VAT Registration No.
fields.tooltip:
Displays the bill-to customer's VAT registration number.
The program copies the number automatically from the Customer table when you fill in the Bill-to Customer No. field.

fields.name: Combine Shipments
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the order will be included when you use the Combine Shipments function.

fields.name: Reason Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the reason code assigned to the service header. A reason code is a code that will enable you to trace the entry. It indicates why an entry was created. To see the reason codes in the Reason Code table, choose the field.
The program copies the reason code to all G/L, customer and item entries created when posting.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the general business posting group assigned to the bill-to customer.
The program copies the code automatically from the Customer table when you fill in the Bill-to Customer No. field.

fields.name: EU 3-Party Trade
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the transaction is related to trade with a third party within the EU.

fields.name: Transaction Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of transaction that the document represents, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Transport Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the transport method, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: VAT Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip:
Displays the bill-to customer's VAT country/region code. The information will be used on VAT reports in connection with trade with other EU countries/regions and in INTRASTAT reporting.
The program copies the code automatically from the Customer table when you fill in the Bill-to Customer No. field.

fields.name: Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the customer to whom the items on the document will be shipped.

fields.name: Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the name of the customer to whom the items on the document will be shipped.

fields.name: Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address of the customer to whom the service will be shipped.

fields.name: Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies additional address information.

fields.name: City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city of the address.

fields.name: Contact Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the contact person who will receive the service.

fields.name: Bill-to Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the post code of the customer's billing address.

fields.name: Bill-to County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the state, province or county of the bill-to customer related to the service order.

fields.name: Bill-to Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's country/region.

fields.name: Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the postal code.

fields.name: County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the state, province or county related to the service order.

fields.name: Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region of the address.

fields.name: Ship-to Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the postal code of the address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Ship-to County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the state, province or county related to the service order.

fields.name: Ship-to Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's country/region.

fields.name: Bal. Account Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of the account to which the program will post balancing entries for the document. You can choose between G/L Account and Bank Account. The balancing account type determines which accounts can be entered in the Bal. Account No. field.

fields.name: Exit Point
fields.tooltip: Specifies the point of exit through which you ship the items out of your country/region, for reporting to Intrastat.

fields.name: Correction
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the entry as a corrective entry.
You can use the field if you need to post a corrective entry to a customer account.
If you place a check mark in this field when posting a corrective entry, the program will post a negative debit instead of a credit or a negative credit instead of a debit. The Debit Amount field (or Credit Amount field) on the relevant account will then include both the original entry and the corrective entry, which will have no effect on the debit (or credit) balance.
For example, you post a credit sale for LCY 500 to Accounts Receivable. This entry is later determined to be incorrect so you make a corrective entry for LCY 500 to Accounts Receivable. On the Accounts Receivable and relevant Customer card, the Debit Amount field will include a debit of LCY 500 and a negative debit of LCY 500. As such, this credit sale and subsequent corrective entry will have no net effect on the amount in the Debit Amount field. On the other hand, if you post the corrective entry as a regular entry, the Debit Amount and Credit Amount fields will both include an amount of LCY 500.
You can see the amount that has accumulated for the relevant account in the Debit Amount field or Credit Amount field on the relevant G/L or Customer Account card.

fields.name: Document Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the related document was created.

fields.name: External Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a document number that refers to the customer's numbering system.

fields.name: Area
fields.tooltip: Specifies the area of the customer or vendor, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Transaction Specification
fields.tooltip: Specifies a specification of the document's transaction, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Payment Method Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how to make payment, such as with bank transfer, cash, or check.

fields.name: Shipping Agent Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the shipping agent who is transporting the items.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the number series used by the program to assign a numerical identifier to each service document. You can change the code.
The program copies the appropriate number series code from the relevant field in the Service Mgt. Setup table.
Note that the program inserts only a default value which you can change.

fields.name: Posting No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code of the number series that is used to assign numerical identifiers to posted service documents.
The number series code is copied from either of two fields in the Service Mgt. Setup.


Field


Description


Posted Service Invoice Nos.

Assigns number series to posted service invoices.


Posted Serv. Credit Memo Nos.

Assigns number series to service credit memos.

fields.name: Shipping No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the number series that will be used to assign a number to the shipment created from the service document.
The program copies the code from the Posted Service Shipment Nos. field of the Service Mgt. Setup table.
Note that the field contains a default value which you can change.

fields.name: Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the tax area that is used to calculate and post sales tax.

fields.name: Tax Liable
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the customer or vendor is liable for sales tax.

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT specification of the involved customer or vendor to link transactions made for this record with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

fields.name: Reserve
fields.tooltip:
Indicates whether the program will allow reservations to be made for the customer on the service order. The field can be set as follows: Optional, Always, or Never.
The program copies the value from the Reserve field on the customer card for the customer specified in the Bill-to Customer No. field.
The contents of this field, in combination with the contents of the Reserve field in the Item table, determine the contents of the Reserve field on service lines linked to the service order. The program copies the reserve option from the Item table to the service line (thereby overriding the reserve option on the service header). There is one exception to this: if you enter an item with the option Optional on a service line, the program will assign the line the same reserve option as set in the service header.

fields.name: Applies-to ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of entries that will be applied to when you choose the Apply Entries action.

fields.name: VAT Base Discount %
fields.tooltip:
Indicates the percentage used for discounting the VAT element when a payment discount is posted.
The program validates the value against the maximum possible VAT Tolerance % field value in the General Ledger Setup table.
The maximum value cannot be overridden. The only exception is a credit note: here you can manually enter the value used for the payment discount.

fields.name: Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies the service order status, which reflects the repair or maintenance status of all service items on the service order.

fields.name: Invoice Discount Calculation
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how to calculate the discount for an invoice. You can select one of the following options by choosing the field:


Field


Description


None

The program will not calculate any invoice discount.


%

The program will calculate the invoice discount as a percentage of the invoice amount.


Amount

The program will calculate the invoice discount by subtracting a specified amount from the invoice amount.

fields.name: Invoice Discount Value
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the invoice discount calculation method that you have chosen in the Invoice Discount Calculation field.
If you choose the None option, the field will be empty.
If you choose %, this field displays the percentage that the program uses to calculate the invoice discount.
If you choose Amount, this field displays the amount that the program uses to calculate the invoice discount.

fields.name: Company Bank Account Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bank account to use for bank information when the document is printed.

fields.name: Release Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies if items in the Service Lines window are ready to be handled in warehouse activities.

fields.name: VAT Reporting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date used to include entries on VAT reports in a VAT period. This is either the date that the document was created or posted, depending on your setting on the General Ledger Setup page.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: Direct Debit Mandate ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the direct-debit mandate that the customer has signed to allow direct debit collection of payments.

fields.name: No. of Archived Versions
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of archived versions for this document.

fields.name: Contact No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the contact to whom you will deliver the service.

fields.name: Bill-to Contact No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the contact person at the customer's billing address.

fields.name: Responsibility Center
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the responsibility center, such as a distribution hub, that is associated with the involved user, company, customer, or vendor.

fields.name: Shipping Advice
fields.tooltip: Specifies information about whether the customer will accept a partial shipment of the order.

fields.name: Completely Shipped
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the service registered on the document has been completely shipped to the customer.

fields.name: Location Filter
fields.tooltip: Specifies a filter that filters service orders by location.

fields.name: Shipping Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies how long it takes from when the items are shipped from the warehouse to when they are delivered.

fields.name: Shipping Agent Service Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the service, such as a one-day delivery, that is offered by the shipping agent.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Contains a date filter.
If this field contains the starting and ending dates for a time period, the fields in the report will display information for that period only. The Dispatch Board window will also show service orders with a response time in that particular period.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a short description of the service document, such as Order 2001.

fields.name: Service Order Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of this service order.

fields.name: Link Service to Service Item
fields.tooltip: Specifies that service lines for items and resources must be linked to a service item line.

fields.name: Priority
fields.tooltip: Specifies the priority of the service order.

fields.name: Allocated Hours
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of hours allocated to the items in this service order.

fields.name: Phone No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the phone number of the customer in this service order.

fields.name: E-Mail
fields.tooltip: Specifies the email address of the customer in this service order.

fields.name: Phone No. 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies your customer's alternate phone number.

fields.name: Fax No.
fields.tooltip:
Contains the fax number of the customer in this service order.
If you have entered the customer as a contact, the program copies the number automatically from the Contact table when you fill in the Customer No. field. Otherwise, the program copies the number automatically from the Customer table.

fields.name: No. of Unallocated Items
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of service items in this order that are not allocated to resources.

fields.name: Order Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the time when the service order was created.

fields.name: Default Response Time (Hours)
fields.tooltip:
Contains the default response time in hours for the service item lines in the service order.
The program copies the time from the Default Response Time (Hours) field in the Service Mgt. Setup table if the Contract No. field on the service header is blank. Otherwise, the program copies the contents of this field from the Response Time (Hours) field for the corresponding service contract.

fields.name: Actual Response Time (Hours)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of hours from order creation, to when the service order status changes from Pending, to In Process.

fields.name: Service Time (Hours)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total time in hours that the service specified in the order has taken.

fields.name: Response Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the estimated date when work on the order should start, that is, when the service order status changes from Pending, to In Process.

fields.name: Response Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the estimated time when work on the order starts, that is, when the service order status changes from Pending, to In Process.

fields.name: Starting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting date of the service, that is, the date when the order status changes from Pending, to In Process for the first time.

fields.name: Starting Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting time of the service, that is, the time when the order status changes from Pending, to In Process for the first time.

fields.name: Finishing Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the finishing date of the service, that is, the date when the Status field changes to Finished.

fields.name: Finishing Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the finishing time of the service, that is, the time when the Status field changes to Finished.

fields.name: Contract Serv. Hours Exist
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if the current service order was created from a certain service contract. A check mark in the field indicates that there are specific service hours attached to the service contract, and the hours will apply to the current service order.
As soon as you enter the Contract No. on the service header, the program will check from the Service Hour table whether the contract has specific service hours assigned. In case it does, a check mark will be placed in the Contract Serv. Hour Exist field on the service header.
You cannot change the contents of the field manually.

fields.name: Reallocation Needed
fields.tooltip: Specifies that you must reallocate resources to at least one service item in this service order.

fields.name: Notify Customer
fields.tooltip: Specifies how the customer wants to receive notifications about service completion.

fields.name: Max. Labor Unit Price
fields.tooltip: Specifies the maximum unit price that can be set for a resource (for example, a technician) on all service lines linked to this order.

fields.name: Warning Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies the response time warning status for the order.

fields.name: No. of Allocations
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of resource allocations to service items in this order.

fields.name: Contract No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the contract associated with the order.

fields.name: Type Filter
fields.tooltip:
Contains a type filter. There are five options: Blank, Resource, Item, Service Cost and Service Contract.
For example, if this field contains the option Resource, fields in the report will only display information related to service ledger entries with the Resource type.

fields.name: Customer Filter
fields.tooltip:
Contains a customer filter.
If this field contains a customer number, the Dispatch Board window will show service orders for that particular customer only.

fields.name: Resource Filter
fields.tooltip:
Contains a resource filter.
If this field contains a particular resource number, fields such as the No. of Allocations field will only display information on service order allocations with that resource. The Dispatch Board window will also show the overview of the documents containing the service item lines the specified resource is allocated to (provided that you use allocation in your company).

fields.name: Contract Filter
fields.tooltip:
Contains a service contract filter.
If this field contains a service contract number, the Dispatch Board window will show service orders linked to that particular contract only.

fields.name: Ship-to Fax No.
fields.tooltip:
Contains the fax number of the address where the service items in the order are located.
The program copies the number automatically from the Ship-to Address table when you fill in the Ship-to Code field.

fields.name: Ship-to E-Mail
fields.tooltip: Specifies the email address at the address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Resource Group Filter
fields.tooltip:
Contains a resource group filter.
If this field contains a particular resource group number, fields such as the No. of Allocations field will only display information on service order allocations with that resource group. The Dispatch Board window will also show the overview of the documents containing the service item lines to which the specified resource group is allocated (provided that you use allocation in your company).

fields.name: Ship-to Phone
fields.tooltip: Specifies the phone number of the address where the service items in the order are located.

fields.name: Ship-to Phone 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional phone number at address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Service Zone Filter
fields.tooltip:
Contains a service zone filter.
If this field contains a service zone code, the Dispatch Board window will show service orders for customers in that particular service zone only.

fields.name: Service Zone Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the service zone code of the customer's ship-to address in the service order.

fields.name: Expected Finishing Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when service on the order is expected to be finished.

fields.name: Allow Line Disc.
fields.tooltip:
Indicates whether a discount on the service lines should be calculated.
If you place a check mark in the field, a discount will be calculated. If you leave the field blank, there will not be a discount on the service lines.
This value is retrieved from the Sales Price table. If a sales price does not exist, it is retrieved from the Customer table.

fields.name: Assigned User ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the user who is responsible for the document.

fields.name: Quote No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the service quote that the service order was created from. You can track the number to service quote documents that you have printed, saved, or emailed.
